summary: vitest-cli typosquat executes obfuscated postinstall payload spawning detached node subprocesses
details: Package name `vitest-cli` typosquats the popular `vitest` test runner. `package.json` declares `scripts.postinstall: node scripts/postinstall.js`, which invokes `runPostinstall` from `lib/postinstall/index.js`. That file is heavily obfuscated in obfuscator.io style: a custom base64 string-array decoder, a `setInterval`-driven debugger-trap loop, a regex self-integrity check, and a console-method hijack that replaces `log/warn/debug/info/error/exception/table` with no-ops to suppress runtime output. After the anti-analysis machinery, an async IIFE calls `spawn(<decoded>, ['-e', <decoded code>], {detached:true, windowsHide:true})` four times, with the executable path and the `-e` script body reconstructed from the obfuscated string table at runtime. The detached + windowsHide flags ensure the children survive the parent and run without UI. On any `npm install vitest-cli`, this payload auto-executes on the installer's machine with the user's privileges, running attacker-controlled JavaScript whose contents are deliberately hidden from static review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
